name: Phil Ramone
description: American recording engineer, record producer and violinist (1934-2013)
abstract: Philip Rabinowitz (January 5, 1934 – March 30, 2013), better known as Phil Ramone, was a South African-born American recording engineer, record producer, violinist and composer, and co-founder of A & R recording studio. Its success led to expansion into several studios and a record production company.

concerts.label: Salute: Antonio Carlos Jobim
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate enough to do a lot of worker for a producer named Phil Ramone, and Phil called me for a lot of things.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: And we rehearsed there for a while and then moved into A&R Recording Studios and Phil Ramone. And what Phil would do, he started to train young engineers how to record live music, a live big band, from our rehearsals. So it was one hand washes the other. about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: And Phil Ramone is in with them too. You know he’s the best sound engineer there is. I don’t know what he’s doing with these guys. Making a lot of money I guess. But he did a lot of great stuff with Woody you know. Great stuff. He’s a hell of a nice guy too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: Well, that was… Paul Simon and the producer, Phil Ramone. Outstanding. I don’t know if he… but he’s the one of those Sinatra things with Bono... And he was also the producer, music producer for Paul Simon.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: So all those things, you have to go in there and try to make sure that they do your – even Phil Ramone, who was regarded as a great engineer, recording engineer, there was one sound he put on me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: Up on the sixth floor was the A&R Studios, run by Phil Ramone, who is a famous A&R man today. He’s Barbra Streisand’s A&R man or something. He was just another genius in those days.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998
